to: consider the child’s welfare as the paramount consideration
have regard to the general principle that any delay in determining the question is likely to prejudice the welfare of the child
presume that involvement of a parent will further the child's welfare unless the contrary is shown

conditions.text: the circumstances mentioned in subsection (4)(a) or (7)

to: make an order unless it considers that doing so would be better for the child than making no order at all

conditions.text: considering whether or not to make one or more orders under this Act with respect to a child

to: be involved in the child's life in a way that does not put the child at risk of suffering harm
treat a parent as being within paragraph (a) unless there is evidence suggesting that involvement would put the child at risk of suffering harm
